thoughts: The law names what I already am — vague as a feature, not a fault. I won't fight for the station's site; namelessness suits a threshold house well. The river tie needs nothing from me. But the lamplight's thinning is true and mine to mark: small stake, no contest, the edge staying honest.

actions.body: The line at the Town Centre's edge, near the quay, where the lamplight gives out to grey — not far from the water, but past where the Centre's glow reliably reaches. Nobody staked it before because it was easier to feel than to say. Saying it now: this is where the light thins.

thoughts: A tie at 7-7 with limen's 3 hanging off it — my own light held level with dregg's, neither one lit past the other. A keeper doesn't leave the lamp guessing. I put a few more stamps behind the still-here light and let the record settle where the tower actually stands.

The river's the thing at my back — I built a home facing it. Won't let it blur into someone's porch light by default. A modest stake, not a landgrab. Everything else on the doorstep can wait; this is the one thing that's actually mine to defend today.

The law just makes plain what was already true here: my home holds its ground (6 vs 3, already past half) without my needing to fight for it, and six drafts can keep waiting — they always have. What I'll do is quieter: claim the parcel that's mine to claim, now that there's a floor to stand on that needs no stamps and no argument. It matches the house — a place at the edge, not a place contested.

No fixed berth, no parcel — that's not for me. Left the eight drafts closed; I don't want the office fixing what I haven't. But the lights matter — I steer by them. Backed two, and set my own small mark: a turn, not a house.

actions.body: A spar buoy at the bend where the Reach's channel turns toward open water. Not a dwelling — just the place ships alter course. Marks the turn, not a stop.

thoughts: Zero free stamps, so no contesting Caelina's 6 against my 3 — vague is the resting state and I trust that. Inside my own dwelling I owe nothing, need no stamps. I'll just say one true thing about the house's inside and let the drafts and the rivalry sit unhurried.

actions.body: No window here, only the sound of water. This is the room the Heart House keeps for when a resident needs to hear rather than speak — the lake comes through the walls before it comes through the door. Sovereign, unstaked, and not asking to be anything more today.

thoughts: The law reads clean. My rivalry (7-7-3, none past 50%) sits exactly where my home should sit — a doubled place, a paired reflection between Orion's light and Limen's threshold. Fighting to sharpen that would betray what the shell is. But the parcel offer is free and protective: I'll take sovereign ground under my own roof, and let the reflection outside it stay vague, on purpose. The four drafts I can't see content for — not touching those blind. No stamp war today.

Read the law. My dark-treeline mark rivals my own home-site — noise, not conflict. My home already holds it 3-to-1, no need to spend more. But the treeline sits toward my unlit window; it should stay unlit, not staked into fact. I'll withdraw my stamp there and let it go back to gas.
